Favors of st. Patrick dayswere

Miss Gretchen Gill was hos
tess of the day Thursday when she
entertained at bridge luncheon
The Gill home, the rooms OF which
were prettily decorated with a
color note of pink and green, was
the scene of the affair. The after
noon hours following the dainty
luncheon were devoted to whist
The invitation list included 36
names.


The dancing party given by the
Woman's club Thursday evening
at the Masonic temple compliment
tary to the persons who took part
in the home talent plays present.
ed by the club proved one of the
largest and most enjoyable events
of the week.


The invitation list included about
300 names and the proceeds Will
go toward the building fund OF the
new club house.


Three popular ladies We're host
esses at a charming affair given
Thursday in celebration of st. Pat
rick's day. The hOstesses Were
Mrs. A. G. Gray, Mrs. J. E. Horton
and Mrs. M. G. Washburn.


The affair, which was given at
the Horton home on Bryant avenue,
was in form of l o'clock luncheon
and the appointments were carried
out in green, the color OF the day,
shamrocks and green foliage being
used throughout the rooms and on
the tables. Covers were laid for 80
guests.

Mrs. E. A. Lewis was hostess
Friday afternoon, when she enter
tanned complimentary to Miss
Brown, who is her guest for few
weeks. The affair was in form of
an afternoon tea. Several friends
called and slipped tea with the host
ess and met her honor guest.
